mission.title: linkerd: Make Prometheus Pluggable
mission.description: Make Prometheus Pluggable. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Verify Linkerd version supports pluggable Prometheus
steps.description:
Ensure you are running Linkerd 2.10 or later, which supports an external Prometheus:
```bash
linkerd version
```
If running an older version, upgrade Linkerd following the official upgrade guide.

steps.title: Set up your external Prometheus instance
steps.description:
Deploy or configure an external Prometheus that will scrape Linkerd metrics. Ensure it is configured to scrape the Linkerd proxy metrics endpoints. Add the following scrape config:
```yaml
- job_name: 'linkerd'
  kubernetes_sd_configs:
  - role: pod
  relabel_configs:
  - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_container_name]
    action: keep
    regex: linkerd-proxy
  - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_container_port_name]
    action: keep
    regex: linkerd-admin
```

steps.title: Install Linkerd Viz with external Prometheus
steps.description:
Install the Linkerd Viz extension pointing to your external Prometheus. This disables the built-in Prometheus:
```bash
linkerd viz install --set prometheusUrl=http://your-prometheus:9090 | kubectl apply -f -
```
Alternatively, via Helm:
```bash
helm install linkerd-viz linkerd/linkerd-viz \
  --set prometheusUrl=http://your-prometheus:9090
```

steps.title: Verify metrics flow through external Prometheus
steps.description:
Confirm that Linkerd dashboards work with the external Prometheus:
```bash
linkerd viz dashboard &
linkerd viz stat deploy -n default
```
Verify that metrics appear in your external Prometheus by querying for `request_total` or `response_latency_ms_bucket` metrics with the `linkerd` job label.

resolution.summary: Starting with Linkerd 2.10, the built-in Prometheus instance is optional. Users can configure Linkerd to send metrics to an external Prometheus by setting `prometheusUrl` in the Linkerd Viz extension. Install without built-in Prometheus: `linkerd viz install --set prometheusUrl=http://your-prometheus:9090 | kubectl apply -f -`.
codeSnippets: linkerd viz install --set prometheusUrl=http://your-prometheus:9090 | kubectl apply -f -

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
